userStories.title: Add Uniswap V3 Swap event indexer
userStories.description: Create a chain-side indexer script that listens to Uniswap V3 Swap events and generates swap summaries, eliminating the manual keeper gap for DEX activity.
acceptanceCriteria: Create `scripts/indexer-uniswap.ts` that indexes Swap events from configured Uniswap pools on X Layer testnet
The indexer outputs swap summaries compatible with `submitSwapSummary` input format
Add a simple integration test or manual verification script showing event capture works
TypeScript compiles without errors (`pnpm tsc --noEmit`)

userStories.title: Create scheduled daemon service for auto-submission
userStories.description: Convert existing keeper scripts into a scheduled daemon that automatically calls `submitSwapSummary`, `submitActivitySummary`, and `submitWalletMeta` at regular intervals.
acceptanceCriteria: Create `scripts/keeper-daemon.ts` that runs on a schedule (e.g. every 5 minutes via `node-cron` or `setInterval`)
Daemon automatically invokes existing keeper logic for swap, activity, and wallet meta submissions
Daemon runs in the target project directory and respects `.env` configuration
Add a README section explaining how to start/stop the daemon
TypeScript compiles without errors

userStories.title: Add retry, deduplication and idempotency to keeper submissions
userStories.description: Prevent duplicate or failed on-chain submissions by adding retry logic and block-range-based deduplication to the keeper daemon.
acceptanceCriteria: Implement exponential backoff retry (up to 3 attempts) for all `submit*` contract calls
Add block-range / nonce deduplication so the same summary is never submitted twice
Persist last successfully submitted block number/timestamp to a local state file
TypeScript compiles without errors; existing tests still pass (`pnpm test`)

userStories.title: Migrate contract governance to Safe multisig
userStories.description: Replace the single EOA governance address with a 2-of-3 Gnosis Safe multisig for all critical governance operations in production.
acceptanceCriteria: Update deployment scripts to accept a `GOVERNANCE_SAFE` address and use it as the initial governance address
Verify that `scheduleRegisterModule`, `executeRegisterModule`, `scheduleUpdateWeight`, `executeUpdateWeight`, and `transferGovernance` require multisig execution
Add a test or script demonstrating a multisig-executed governance flow (can use a mock multisig or local Safe)
Solidity tests pass (`forge test`)

userStories.title: Implement wash trade (A→B→A) detection in indexer
userStories.description: Detect closed-loop swap cycles within 10 blocks from the same wallet and flag them as wash trades before on-chain submission.
acceptanceCriteria: Add wash-trade detection logic to `scripts/indexer-uniswap.ts` (or a shared util)
Flag is set when the same wallet performs A→B swap followed by B→A swap within 10 blocks
Flagged summaries include `washTradeFlag: true` and are still submitted but marked for scoring penalties
Add a unit test for the detection logic
TypeScript compiles without errors

userStories.title: Implement counterparty concentration check in indexer
userStories.description: Detect sybil behavior by checking if >70% of a wallet's swap activity involves the same 2 counterparties.
acceptanceCriteria: Add counterparty concentration logic to the indexer or a shared analysis util
Trigger a penalty flag when >70% of swaps in a window involve ≤2 unique counterparties
Integrate the flag into the summary submitted to the validator
Add a unit test for the concentration logic
TypeScript compiles without errors

userStories.title: Add Slither SAST to GitHub Actions CI
userStories.description: Run Slither static analysis on every PR to catch high-risk Solidity vulnerability patterns automatically.
acceptanceCriteria: Extend `.github/workflows/test.yml` with a Slither analysis step
CI installs Slither (via `pip install slither-analyzer` or a pre-built action)
The workflow fails on high-severity findings (configurable)
A test push shows the Slither step running in CI

userStories.title: Add EIP-712 signature verification for keeper submissions
userStories.description: Require keepers to sign summary data with EIP-712 before submitting on-chain, reducing the risk of a leaked keeper key.
acceptanceCriteria: Define an EIP-712 domain and type hash for swap/activity/wallet meta summaries
Update `AgentRepValidator` `submit*` functions to verify the keeper's signature instead of just `onlyKeeper`
Update keeper scripts to sign payloads before submission
Add Foundry tests verifying valid signatures are accepted and invalid ones are rejected
Solidity tests pass (`forge test`)

userStories.title: Add structured JSON logging to skill and keeper layers
userStories.description: Replace console.log with structured JSON logging in the skill API and keeper daemon for observability and log aggregation.
acceptanceCriteria: Add a lightweight logger (e.g. `pino` or a simple wrapper) to `src/` and `scripts/`
All skill commands and keeper scripts output JSON-structured logs with level, message, and metadata
Logs include `error` objects with stack traces when exceptions occur
TypeScript compiles without errors; existing tests still pass

userStories.title: Add Uniswap slot0 price sanity check to evaluation
userStories.description: Enhance UniswapScoreModule.evaluate by reading the pool's slot0 sqrtPriceX96 to perform a secondary price-reasonableness check.
acceptanceCriteria: Update `UniswapScoreModule.sol` to read `slot0` from the referenced Uniswap pool during evaluation
Implement a price deviation check (e.g. reject if implied price deviates >X% from expected range)
Update ABI files in `src/skill/abis.ts` if new view functions are needed
Add/update Foundry tests for the new validation path
Solidity tests pass (`forge test`)

userStories.title: Add keeper health check and basic monitoring
userStories.description: Expose a lightweight health check endpoint or log heartbeat so operators can detect when the keeper service stalls.
acceptanceCriteria: The daemon writes a heartbeat log or file (`keeper-health.json`) with `lastSuccessBlock` and `lastSuccessTimestamp` after each successful submission round
If a submission fails for 3 consecutive rounds, emit an `ERROR` level log with explicit alert text
Add a simple CLI check command (e.g. `pnpm keeper:health`) that reads the health file and exits 0/1
TypeScript compiles without errors
